title: HubSpot API rate limit exceeded
symptom: API calls return HTTP 429 Too Many Requests. Batch operations or rapid sequential calls fail after initial successes.
applies_when: Making many API calls in a short period
Using batch endpoints or looping over records
not_this_if: Error is 403 (scope/permission issue, not rate limit)
Only a single API call fails
root_cause: HubSpot enforces rate limits: 100 requests per 10 seconds for OAuth apps, 200 for private apps. Burst operations like syncing many records or creating deals in a loop quickly hit this limit.
fix_steps: Add retry logic with exponential backoff (start at 1s, max 30s)
Check the Retry-After header in the 429 response for exact wait time
Batch operations: use HubSpot batch endpoints instead of individual calls
Reduce concurrency to 5-10 parallel requests maximum
agent_instruction: Implement exponential backoff retry logic for the HubSpot API call. Use the Retry-After header value. If doing bulk operations, switch to HubSpot batch endpoints.
version_notes: Rate limits differ between OAuth apps (100/10s) and private apps (200/10s)
